question_text: Does IPaC answer a real external need, or primarily an internal intellectual/systemic pain?
why_it_matters: If the need is not externally recognizable, IPaC may remain internally coherent but externally weak.
risk_if_ignored: False maturity; solution self-justification; weak adoption.

question_text: Can living meaning be translated into manageable artifacts without killing its living core?
related_concept: Living Meaning; Knowledge Artifact; Knowledge Clump
why_it_matters: This is the central transformation claim of IPaC.
risk_if_ignored: Artifacts become dead documentation; the core need is betrayed.

question_text: What minimal structure is required for a Knowledge Clump to remain alive and portable?
why_it_matters: Too little structure loses context; too much structure creates bureaucracy.
risk_if_ignored: Either context collapse or document proliferation.

question_text: Is the Canon / Trace / Review / Decision / Controlled Rebuild circuit actually workable in practice?
related_concept: Canon; Trace; Review; Decision; Controlled Rebuild
why_it_matters: This is the stable living knowledge circuit claimed by the architecture invariants.
risk_if_ignored: The architecture remains internally coherent but operationally untested.

question_text: How to prevent Review / Decision / Trace discipline from becoming bureaucracy?
why_it_matters: The same discipline that prevents false canonization may slow or deaden the system.
risk_if_ignored: Process overload; living knowledge becomes dead procedure.

question_text: Can Human + AI collaboration reliably hold complexity better than an ordinary long chat?
why_it_matters: IPaC relies on AI as methodological support without transferring subjecthood.
risk_if_ignored: AI over-synthesis, yes-man drift, false coherence.

question_text: Can the AI Mentor Layer transfer the working mode to a new Project or agent?
why_it_matters: Portation depends on transferring not only files but working discipline.
risk_if_ignored: New Project starts from zero or becomes generic.

question_text: Can Obsidian / Git / Project serve as stable memory without creating tool capture?
why_it_matters: The tools may support the system, but must not become the concept.
risk_if_ignored: Tool-driven conclusions; Obsidian/Git mistaken for IPaC.

question_text: What is the boundary between IPaC and Knowledge Engineering?
why_it_matters: Without boundary work, IPaC may duplicate existing fields under new wording.
risk_if_ignored: False novelty; weak positioning; conceptual inflation.

question_text: What is the boundary between IPaC and Context Engineering?
why_it_matters: Context Engineering may overlap with IPaC boot/context-pack logic.
risk_if_ignored: Terminology fog; duplicated field claims.

question_text: Does the phrase Meaning Bottleneck travel outside the internal project context?
why_it_matters: It was selected as a key conceptual entry point but has no field evidence.
risk_if_ignored: Beautiful formulation treated as validation.

question_text: Does the term Knowledge Clump make sense to external reviewers?
why_it_matters: The term is central internally but may not be portable externally.
risk_if_ignored: Terminology fog; weak communication; concept rejection due to naming.

question_text: How to distinguish research support from validation in daily project work?
why_it_matters: Phase 5 explicitly states research support is not validation.
risk_if_ignored: False maturity and premature canonization.

question_text: What field evidence would be sufficient to upgrade core hypotheses?
why_it_matters: The project needs clear validation thresholds before public or architectural claims.
risk_if_ignored: Hypothesis status drift and overclaiming.

question_text: Can field feedback be turned into controlled rebuild rather than chaos?
why_it_matters: The living knowledge cycle depends on feedback improving the system without destabilizing canon.
risk_if_ignored: Feedback panic, opportunistic rewriting, canon instability.

question_text: Which parts of the former execution work are parked signals and which are reusable mechanisms?
why_it_matters: Earlier execution artifacts are valuable but not architecture.
risk_if_ignored: Execution candidates re-enter architecture core.

question_text: When should Codex / execution agents be connected without damaging concept boundaries?
why_it_matters: Codex may support implementation but can also cause execution pull.
risk_if_ignored: Technical implementation starts before conceptual and validation discipline is stable.

question_text: Does IPaC require RAG / Knowledge Graph / Neo4j, or are these only possible tools?
why_it_matters: Avoid tool capture and premature technical architecture.
risk_if_ignored: Technical stack becomes concept substitute.

question_text: Which terms are working terms and which are public terms?
related_concept: Living Meaning; Knowledge Clump; Canon; Resonance; Intersubjectivity
why_it_matters: Internal terms may be powerful but externally unclear.
risk_if_ignored: Terminology fog, rejection, or pseudo-philosophical impression.

question_text: What should be parked until after Wave 2 closure?
why_it_matters: Not all important questions should be solved now.
risk_if_ignored: Research-as-delay and execution pull.

question_text: Can IPaC be explained without excessive simplification?
why_it_matters: Public understanding may require simplification, but simplification may distort the concept.
risk_if_ignored: Either opacity or flattening into marketing/content workflow.

question_text: What is the next correct phase after Phase 6?
why_it_matters: The prompt specifies Wave 2 Closure / Knowledge Recovery Pack Assembly.
risk_if_ignored: The project may keep producing isolated phase artifacts instead of packaging recovery state.
